National Skills Needs List Script/User’s Choice
Compliance Requirements
1) 	You may not use the word, FREE, to describe this program
2) 	You may use the phrase “100% government funding,” but not “100 state
funding,” as we are using a combination of state and federal funding (The
actual funding % is greater than 100%, due to the incentives)
3) 	You must disclose the (approximately) $600 student fee, which can be paid up
front or upon receiving their incentives.
Hey, my name is ________________? 	From (your companies name)?
Who’s the right person to speak to here about employee training? Would that
be you? The head of HR? Someone else?
Okay, great! Well, mate, the reason for the call today is that we work in
conjunction with the state government … and, right now, __________, is on
the national skills needs list—priority one—which means there’s 100%
funding available, plus huge cash incentives, to get your workers their
national 	____________ 	qualification. 	Mate, 	are 	you 	familiar 	with 	the
government funding that’s available right now?
Okay, great! Well, mate, all I’d like to do right now, with your permission, of
course, is send you a bit of information on my company, (your companies
name), via email, along with a quick explanation of how the government
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

funding works, and then get back to you in the next few days to discuss
things in more detail. Sound fair enough?
Okay, great. Just ask a couple of quick questions so I can tailor the email to
best suit your needs.
1) Now, first, how many __________ do you have out in the field right
now, just a ballpark?
2) And what’s their current employment status? Are they permanent,
either part time or full time? Or are they casual? Or any subbies out
there?
3) And 	are 	there 	any 	other 	workers 	out 	there, 	supporting 	your
_________. 	like 	drivers 	or 	_______ 	or 	people 	working 	in 	the
warehouse?
4) And is this your only business, mate, or do you have other divisions?
Okay, great. Well, ________, based on everything you just said to me, this is
definitely a perfect fit for you. So let me get that email out to you straight
away and then I’ll get back to you in a few days and we can discuss things in
more detail. Sound fair enough?
Great. Now, what’s the best email to reach you on?
And what’s the best time to typically speak to you? Mornings … afternoons?
I’m flexible either way.
Okay, excellent. And is this the best number to reach on?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 3 --

Okay, great, mate. Again, my name is _________, and my company is (your
companies name). I’ll get that information out to you straight away, and I
look forward to speaking to you in the next couple of days. Sound good?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 3 --
